name: Muros de Nalón, Quinta de Selgas y Playa de Aguilar — circular desde Espíritu Santo
region_intro: Touring cycling around Muros de Nalón offers routes through a picturesque coastal municipality in Asturias, characterized by lush, verdant scenery and rugged coastal cliffs. The region features diverse landscapes including sandy beaches, the Nalón River estuary, and extensive forests of pine and eucalyptus. Cycling routes often follow the dramatic coastline, providing views of the Cantabrian Sea, and traverse river valleys. Best touring cycling routes around Muros De Nalón The most popular touring cycling route is Muros de Nalón, Quinta de Selgas y Playa de Aguilar — circular desde Espíritu Santo, a 10.0 miles (16.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 7 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers coastal views and passes by Playa del Aguilar. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Bridge over the Nalón River – Muros de Nalón loop from San Esteban de Pravia, a difficult 38.8 miles (62.5 km) path. This route explores the Nalón River and its surrounding valleys. Local touring cyclists also love the Quinta de Selgas – Muros de Nalón loop from Muros de Nalon, a 24.6 miles (39.6 km) trail leading through the coastal municipality, often completed in about 2 hours 48 minutes. Touring cycling around Muros de Nalón is defined by coastal paths, river estuaries, and shaded forests. The network offers options for moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in Muros de Nalón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Muros de Nalón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Muros de Nalón?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes around Muros de Nalón, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. You can find options ranging from moderate to difficult.

Q: What are the typical terrain and difficulty levels for touring cycling in Muros de Nalón?
A: Muros de Nalón offers a mix of moderate and difficult touring cycling routes. The terrain often includes coastal paths with dramatic cliffs, river estuaries, and shaded forests. Routes can feature significant elevation changes, especially those exploring the river valleys or extending further into Asturias.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy about Muros de Nalón?
A: The touring cycling routes in Muros de Nalón are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 60 reviews. Cyclists often praise the stunning coastal views, the lush 'Green Spain' scenery, and the diverse landscapes that include beaches, riverbanks, and forests.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Muros de Nalón?
A: Muros de Nalón, part of 'Green Spain,' generally offers pleasant cycling conditions due to its mild climate. Spring and autumn are ideal, with comfortable temperatures and lush scenery. Summer is also popular, especially for coastal rides, though it can be busier. Winters are mild but can be wetter.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Muros de Nalón?
A: Yes, Muros de Nalón features several circular touring cycling routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1029989151/muros-de-nalon-quinta-de-selgas-y-playa-de-aguilar-circular-desde-espiritu-santo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muros de Nalón, Quinta de Selgas y Playa de Aguilar — circular desde Espíritu Santo&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 10-mile loop offering coastal views and passing by Playa del Aguilar. Another option is the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40320708" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge over the Nalón River – Muros de Nalón loop from Muros de Nalon&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on a touring cycling route in Muros de Nalón?
A: You can expect to encounter a rich variety of natural features. Routes often trace the dramatic coastline, offering breathtaking views of the Cantabrian Sea and its impressive cliffs. You'll also find beautiful beaches like Playa del Aguilar, the serene Nalón River estuary, and extensive forests of pine and eucalyptus, providing shaded sections for your ride.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Muros de Nalón?
A: While Muros de Nalón's routes are generally moderate to difficult, some sections of coastal paths or riverbank trails might be suitable for families, especially those with older children or using child seats/trailers. The region's 'Green Spain' character ensures scenic, often shaded, environments. Always check the route's difficulty and elevation profile before heading out with family.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I visit while cycling in Muros de Nalón?
A: As you cycle through Muros de Nalón, you can explore several interesting landmarks. Consider visiting the 15th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353344" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Selgas Palace and the Jesús Nazareno Church‑Pantheon&lt;/a&gt;, or the broader &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/426621" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quinta de Selgas&lt;/a&gt; estate. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2005855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Concha de Artedo Viaduct&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable structure crossing river valleys, offering an interesting architectural sight.

Q: Where can I find parking for my touring cycling trip in Muros de Nalón?
A: Parking is generally available in and around the main town of Muros de Nalón and near popular beaches like Playa del Aguilar. Look for designated parking areas, especially at trailheads or viewpoints, to ensure convenient access to your chosen cycling route.

Q: Is public transport available to access touring cycling routes in Muros de Nalón?
A: Muros de Nalón is accessible by public transport, primarily buses, which connect it to larger towns in Asturias. While direct access to specific trailheads with your bike might require some planning, you can use public transport to reach the municipality and then start your cycling adventure from there.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or accommodation for cyclists in Muros de Nalón?
A: Yes, Muros de Nalón offers various options for refreshments and accommodation. The town itself has cafes, restaurants, and bars where you can refuel. You'll also find a selection of hotels and guesthouses that cater to visitors, some of which may be bike-friendly. Along popular routes, you might encounter smaller establishments in villages or near beaches.

Q: Are there specific routes that offer panoramic coastal views?
A: Absolutely. The dramatic coastline of Muros de Nalón is a highlight for touring cyclists. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1029989151/muros-de-nalon-quinta-de-selgas-y-playa-de-aguilar-circular-desde-espiritu-santo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muros de Nalón, Quinta de Selgas y Playa de Aguilar — circular desde Espíritu Santo&lt;/a&gt; offer stunning vistas of the Cantabrian Sea and its impressive cliffs, including the popular Mirador del Espíritu Santo viewpoint.
tips: A small coastal municipality of just over 1,800 inhabitants. It is worth seeing the stately town of Muros (of Roman and medieval origin), the walls of the old Palace of Valdecarzana and the Palace of the Marqués de Muros, and its old mansions and beautiful buildings.

The Quinta de Selgas is a 19th century palace built on the initiative of the brothers Ezequiel and Fortunato de Selgas Albuerne. Those who wanted to have a palace in the Asturian lands in the Italian way and endow it with important artistic pieces, furniture and household items from renowned houses. Its collection includes pieces by Goya and El Greco. It also has an outstanding collection of tapestries, porcelain and glassware. The entire property is surrounded by a wall and has its own church and a beautifully landscaped garden.
The presence of enormous, beautiful rocks on the coast gives this beach, the most occupied in the municipality of Muros de Nalón, a great attraction.
